# US Stock Watcher Next-Session Forecast — Monday 7/13

**[Monday 7/13 session outlook] Bullish lean — Strong expansion / bull · composite +37 · confidence 48%**

**🔮 Next-session forecast:** The next U.S. regular session is Monday, July 13, opening at 9:30 AM ET (22:30 KST on 7/13).

*Data as of: 2026-07-10T22:16:57 · Data quality: fresh*

## Executive Summary

Looking ahead to the next U.S. regular session — Monday, July 13, opening at 9:30 AM ET (22:30 KST 7/13) — this brief lays out our forecast for that trading day. The overall market-state score stands at +37 on a 100-point scale, placing conditions firmly in "Strong expansion / bull" territory, with a confidence reading of 48% built on four measured components. It's worth noting that several inputs remain unmeasured for this cycle — Liquidity, Credit, Earnings, Macro surprises, Valuation, and Positioning have all been reweighted out of the current composite — so the signal, while constructive, should be read with that caveat in mind.

## Market Diagnosis

**Indices.** Heading into Monday's open, the major benchmarks carry forward a broadly positive tape: the S&P 500 closed at 7,575.39 (+0.42% on the day), the Nasdaq-100 finished at 29,825.11 (+0.33%), the Dow settled at 52,637.01 (+0.29%), while the Russell 2000 lagged with a decline to 2,977.81 (-0.49%) — a divergence that flags softer participation among small-caps even as large-cap benchmarks advanced.

**Market drivers.** Three of the four tracked drivers are currently supportive of the bullish setup: volatility, index trend, and the yield curve are all reading as tailwinds heading into the new week. The lone headwind is the US dollar, which continues to work against the broader risk-on narrative.

**Breadth & concentration.** The composite market-state score of +37/100 corroborates the "Strong expansion / bull" characterization, though the 48% confidence level — derived from just four measured components — suggests this reading carries more uncertainty than a fully-populated model would. As noted above, Liquidity, Credit, Earnings, Macro surprises, Valuation, and Positioning are all sitting outside the current measurement set.

**Sector & rotation.** Rotation into Monday's session favors Financials, Health Care, and Industrials, which stand out as the leadership cohort. On the other side of the ledger, Consumer Staples, Real Estate, and Energy are lagging, consistent with a market still leaning into cyclical and growth-adjacent exposure over defensives.

**Rates, dollar & commodities.** The macro backdrop shows the 10-year Treasury yield at 4.54%, the dollar index (DXY) at 100.97 (+0.02% on the day), WTI crude at $71.51 (-0.79%), and gold at $4,128.90 (-0.04%). Together these inputs paint a relatively stable rates and commodities picture heading into the new trading week, with only modest single-day moves across the complex.

## Outlook

**Short term.** For the upcoming Monday, July 13 session (opening 9:30 AM ET / 22:30 KST 7/13), the near-term bias is constructive, consistent with the "Strong expansion / bull" market-state reading and its 48% confidence level. That said, this view is conditional: it would flip should the overall composite score cross back below zero.

**Medium term.** The medium-term stance carries the same constructive bias, again anchored to the "Strong expansion / bull" state and 48% confidence reading, with the identical trigger for reassessment — a move of the overall score through the zero line would invalidate the current lean.

**Medium-to-long term.** Extending further out, the medium-to-long-term outlook maintains a constructive bias on the same basis — the "Strong expansion / bull" designation at 48% confidence — subject once more to the same flip condition if the composite score crosses zero.
